reviews.review_msg: Loved her teaching style :D would definitely take a class with her again

She truly helped me understand the contents of the course. Her exams were fair and she had engaging Kahoots that helped with cementing knowledge. Very nice and a great prof! Looking to take another class with her if possible.

Course average was a D. Very difficult course. Studied for weeks for the final exam and still did terribly. Good luck to anyone who takes this course with Professor Coates - you will need it (unless you have a photographic memory). Even Gregory House could not have passed that final.
Good lecturer and made engaging Kahoots. Exams were fairly difficult, and grading was unreasonable. Debating received grades was made impossible due to some unfair rules.

I don't get why the reviews are so bad I agree that hinted topics weren't on exams and the written midterm questions felt less relevant, which was frustrating. But it was easy to tell which topics were important. The midterms were tough but the final was fair. It's a tough course that requires like at least 6 hours of studying per week.

No. Just no. I signed up for intro to human physiology, NOT MED SCHOOL. "Know the 12 cranial nerves". Nothing tested! Maybe go to tutorial and clear some of the content up. NOPE. Get SLAMMED with topics you have never heard of before and don't even understand after tutorial or lecture. AVOID. Take it with Nadine to save your sanity.

Where to start... prof seemed nice. In 3 years of being at SFU, never had a prof go out of their way to say in lecture as a hint "important to know for a test" and then literally make sure that's not on the exam at all.. He does show a bit of ego. Clear he intended to trick students in lecture, there should be more lower reviews.

idk what to say, nice personality, but can't teach for shit. Gave us "incentives" for coming to class which were these mini hints, and only one appeared as a mc. Idk if this guy has a compulsive lying habit, but why bluff about hints, if there not gonna be on the exam. Dramatic ass even turned off the mic to make it seem deep. And the marking holy

He's a nice guy but mediocre prof at most. He was obsessed with BPK 305 teaching a 2nd year course and it was impossible to prepare for his exams. The tutorials were mandatory but completely useless, and his lectures specified absolutely nothing for what we would be evaluated on. Stay far away from this guy.

just to preface i have a 3.8GPA and im taking all third year courses rn except this one. ABSOLUTELY HORRENDOUS PROF. nothing prepares you for the exams, i swear they pull the grading scheme out of their asses. he made an INTRO to phys class feel like medical school, in the WORST way. i dont understand why the worst professors teach the most....

He is a nice guy don't get me wrong. But i feel like he purposely makes a point about certain concepts and hints them to be on the exams but then those topics are never seen on the exam. It's also such a strict rubric for what he wants for your answers but then you only have 50 minutes to do the exam so it feels like too much.

She is helpful, clear and makes the class fun. Her notes are easy and to the point. Her first midterm was a little hard, the second midterm was easy, and  final was not easy and not hard. Tutorial quizzes were easy.

Gina is the best prof I have ever had. She is crystal clear and is very helpful in anyt questions you may have. Exams are fair not too hard as long as you keep up with  the notes. TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HER!

inconsistent with her exams. First midterm was extremely hard, second exam was extremely easy. SO you have no idea what her final will be like. And confusing. She would email us after classes correcting her statements and students would correct her iclicker answers (just prior to exams). However, she's very nice and approachable
